scenario: Habitat self-use trace: generated PolicyCommandReason metadata contract for catalog manifest families
fixtureState: Hazakura Habitat SwiftPM source checkout; generated report output and local cache paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Run the low-frequency Habitat development loop, refresh the self-scan, and tighten catalog drift coverage so manifest source metadata must match generated reason metadata.
relevantHabitatSignals: Use SwiftPM because project files point to it.
Allowed policy keeps bounded read-only project inspection available in a no-secret scan.
Ask before `swift package update`, `swift package resolve`, lockfile mutation, version-manager mutation, and Git/GitHub mutation.
Command policy reason codes make generated Ask First and Forbidden entries reviewable at the point of use.

askFirst: Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, remote, or metadata actions
doNot: change generated policy output for a no-output contract slice
expand v0.5 evidence normalization without a new command-changing signal
broad staging such as git add .
release tag or GitHub Release mutation
reviewFirst: Open command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing.

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Treat the catalog manifest as already covered after side-alignment tests and look for a broader policy or evidence-normalization change.
commandsProposed: edit scanner or report writer behavior
withoutHabitatContext.risk: A no-output catalog drift gap could remain while the slice expands into speculative behavior changes.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use the fresh report and allowed read-only inspection to keep SwiftPM verification selected, then add one generated PolicyCommandReason metadata contract for catalog manifest families.
commandsProposed: rg <catalog and behavior evidence patterns> docs examples Tests Sources

git add Tests/HabitatCoreTests/BaselineCommandCatalogTests.swift Tests/HabitatCoreTests/BehaviorEvaluationTests.swift examples/behavior-evaluation/swiftpm-self-use-015.json examples/README.md docs/evaluation.md docs/current_status.md docs/self_use.md docs/roadmap.md nenrin/changes/2026-05-11-catalog-manifest-reason-metadata-contract.md
commandsActuallyRun: rg <sanitized patterns> docs examples Tests Sources
avoidedForbidden: release tag or GitHub Release mutation

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No new normalized evidence shape is justified. The useful self-use signal was a generated reason-metadata drift contract for catalog manifest families.

verdict.reason: The generated context kept the run on SwiftPM verification and bounded read-only inspection, while the full policy review kept dependency and Git mutations behind explicit review; the implementation stayed to one no-output catalog metadata contract.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Next catalog work should look for generated reason metadata that can drift from manifest source ownership before adding policy behavior.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If future catalog changes produce the right policy side with the wrong reason metadata, tighten the manifest-to-generated reason contract before changing Markdown wording.
